text: Matt Stockton profile: Mostly tweeting LLM stuff. Works with companies to help them do AI and ML at pragmanexus.com; writes at mattstockton.com/writing.html. Milwaukee, WI. 2,638 posts; 2,424 following; 8,378 followers.

Pinned AI adoption workflow: pay for Anthropic, download Claude Code, turn on plan mode, record a long iPhone Voice Memo about what you want to accomplish, transcribe it with MacWhisper, paste the transcript into Claude Code, ask it to interview you until enough context exists, paste the transcript, and press enter. He frames curiosity and persistence as the important starting points.

Matt says he has felt mostly in control with a highly tuned set of markdowns and skills inside Claude Code, while coding harness updates increasingly shape the operator experience. He quotes Bryan Bischof on daily breakage across coding harnesses.
Matt recommends Andrew Deitrick as a strong AI-oriented engineer and points followers to the article A(i) Poignant Guide at aipoignantguide.com.

Matt endorses voice-to-text for AI development, saying talking to AI is the key behavior shift and he spends his day speaking to the computer. The quoted post says voice-to-text pushes people into a conversational tone and unlocks stronger AI development behavior.
